explanation: The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV Mode and a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity (ITS_PSV) is detected, then a new inspiratory phase shall be initiated. This captures the requirement that a new inspiration is triggered by the pressure drop while in PSV mode.

The property states that in PSV Mode, a new inspiration shall be initiated immediately upon detection of a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity. The LTL formula "G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))" universally ensures that if the system is in PSV Mode (bool_exp1) and a sudden pressure drop (bool_exp2) occurs, then an inspiratory phase starts (bool_exp3) in the very next state.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PSV Mode and there is a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity (ITS_PSV), a new inspiratory phase must be initiated. This directly translates the causal relationship described in the natural language into an LTL implication where the combined conditions (PSV Mode and pressure drop) imply the initiation of inspiration.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) Mode and a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity occurs (which is the condition for initiating inspiration in PSV mode), then in the next state, a new inspiratory phase shall begin. This captures the causal relationship where the pressure drop acts as the trigger for inspiration in PSV mode.

The property states that globally (always), if the system is in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) Mode AND a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity occurs, then in the very next state, a new inspiratory phase shall begin. This captures the immediate initiation of inspiration by the specified pressure drop within the PSV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode (bool_exp2) and a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity occurs (bool_exp2), then in the very next state (X), a new inspiration shall be initiated (bool_exp3). This directly translates "A new inspiration shall be initiated by a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity (ITS_PSV ), which shall be set by the user (i.e., it yields the condition drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV ))" in the context of “PSV Mode” into an LTL formula, ensuring the immediate causality implied by "initiated by".

The property states that globally, whenever a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity in PSV mode (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV) occurs, a new inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseStart) will eventually be initiated. The atomic proposition 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV' inherently includes the condition of being in 'PSV Mode', as specified by its description.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV Mode AND a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity (ITS_PSV) is detected, then a new inspiratory phase shall be initiated in the very next state. This captures the causal relationship and the context described in the natural language.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in PSV mode (PSVMode) AND a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity occurs (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV), then a new inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseStart) shall be initiated in the very next state (X). This directly captures the immediate cause-and-effect relationship described: a specific pressure drop acts as a trigger for a new inspiration when in PSV mode.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PSV mode (PSVModeSelected) AND a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity (ITS_PSV) is detected (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV), then in the very next state (X), a new inspiratory phase shall begin (inspiratoryPhaseStart). This captures the causal relationship where the pressure drop in PSV mode initiates a new inspiration.
